---
title: "BCB5460 R_Assignment"
author: "Yining Fu"
output: pdf_document
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%Y-%m-%d')`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
rm(list = ls())

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

## File Inspection
### fang_et_al_genotypes.txt

```{r}
# load dataframe using relative path
fang <- read.csv("Input_files/fang_et_al_genotypes.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")

# it has 2782 rows and 986 columns (row number excluding header)
dim(fang)

# header contains "Sample_ID", "JG_OTU", "Group", and all SNP names 
col <- fang[1,]
col[1:10]

# file size
file.size("Input_files/fang_et_al_genotypes.txt")

```

### snp_position.txt

```{r}
# load dataframe using relative path
snp <- read.csv("Input_files/snp_position.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")

# it has 983 rows and 15 columns (row number excluding header)
dim(snp)

# header contains "SNP_ID", "Chromosome", Position", and more shown in standard out
col <- snp[1,]
col[1:10]

# file size
file.size("Input_files/snp_position.txt")

```

## Data Processing 

extract maize and teosinte data from `fang`; change the `Sample_ID` header to `SNP_ID`; delete columns "JG_OTU" and "Group"
```{r}
maize <- fang %>% filter(.[[3]] %in% c("ZMMIL", "ZMMLR", "ZMMMR", "Group"))
teosinte <- fang %>% filter(.[[3]] %in% c("ZMPBA", "ZMPIL", "ZMPJA", "Group"))

maize[1,1] <- "SNP_ID"
teosinte[1,1] <- "SNP_ID"

maize <- maize %>% select(-c(2,3))
teosinte <- teosinte %>% select(-c(2,3))

```

transpose maize and teosinte data
```{r}
tp_maize <- as.data.frame(t(maize))
tp_teosinte <- as.data.frame(t(teosinte))

```

trim `snp` so that it only has columns "SNP_ID", "Chromosome", and "Position"
```{r}
trim_snp <- snp %>% select(c(1,3,4))

```

merge `trim_snp` with `tp_maize` and `tp_teosinte`; note that the header is no longer at the top. Save the header as an object to add back to the output files in the end because it is going to be lost in following steps
```{r}
join_maize <- merge(trim_snp,tp_maize, by.x = 1, by.y = 1)
join_teosinte <- merge(trim_snp,tp_teosinte, by.x = 1, by.y = 1)

header1 <- join_maize %>% filter(.[[1]] %in% c("SNP_ID"))
header2 <- join_teosinte %>% filter(.[[1]] %in% c("SNP_ID"))

```

make three files from `join_maize` with values for position, unknown positions (up), and multiple positions (mp). Header is add back to "maize_up" and "maize_mp"
```{r}
SeparatePosition <- function(file) {
  file_p <- file %>% filter(!.[[2]] %in% c("unknown", "multiple") 
                                 & !.[[3]] %in% c("unknown", "multiple","Position"))
  file_up <- file %>% filter(.[[2]] %in% c("unknown") | .[[3]] %in% c("unknown" )) 
  file_mp <- file %>% filter(.[[2]] %in% c("multiple") | .[[3]] %in% c("multiple")) 
  return(list(file_p, file_up, file_mp))
}

results <- SeparatePosition(join_maize)
maize_p <- as.data.frame(results[1])
maize_up <- as.data.frame(results[2])
maize_mp <- as.data.frame(results[3])

colnames(maize_up) <- header1
colnames(maize_mp) <- header1

write_delim(maize_up, file = paste0("Output_files/maize_up.csv"), delim = "\t")
write_delim(maize_mp, file = paste0("Output_files/maize_mp.csv"), delim = "\t")

```

Separate `maize_p` with chromosome number and sort in increasing and decreasing order (Note: it is sorted numerically but not in a mathematically correct way, e.g. 9300391 > 88863284 because 9>8 regardless of the number of digits). Replace "?" to "-" in the decreasing-order files. Header is added back to the files in the "OutputFile()" function
```{r}
Separate_Sort <- function(file) {
  # separate with chr number
  chr <- list()
  for (i in 1:10) {
    file_i <- file %>% filter(.[[2]] %in% c(i))
    chr[[i]] <- file_i
  }
  # sort in increasing order
  inc <- list()
  for (i in 1:10) {
    i_sort <- chr[[i]] %>% arrange(as.numeric(.[[3]]))
    inc[[i]] <- i_sort
  }
  # sort in descending order and replace ? with -
  dec <- list()
  for (i in 1:10) {
    d_sort <- chr[[i]] %>% arrange(desc(as.numeric(.[[3]])))
    d_sort[d_sort == "?/?"] <- "-/-"
    dec[[i]] <- d_sort
  }
  output <- list(inc,dec)
  return(output)
}


OutputFile <- function(file) {
  results <- Separate_Sort(file)
  inc_file <- results[[1]]
  for (i in 1:10) {
    chri <- as.data.frame(inc_file[i])
    # maize and teosinte data have different number of columns, so different header
    if (species == "maize") {
      colnames(chri) <- header1
    } else {
      colnames(chri) <- header2
    }
    
    filename <- paste0(species,c("_chr"),i,c("_in.csv"))
    write_delim(chri, file = paste0("Output_files/",filename), delim = "\t")
  }
  
  dec_file <- results[[2]]
  for (i in 1:10) {
    chri <- as.data.frame(dec_file[i])
    # again, different header for maize and teosinte
    if (species == "maize") {
      colnames(chri) <- header1
    } else {
      colnames(chri) <- header2
    }
    
    filename <- paste0(species,c("_chr"),i,c("_de.csv"))
    write_delim(chri, file = paste0("Output_files/",filename), delim = "\t")
  }
}

species <- "maize"
OutputFile(maize_p)

```

For teosinte data, repeat the process of making files that have position value and have unknown or multiple positions, separating by chromosome number, sorting based on position value, replace "?" with "-", and output results to directory 
```{r}
results <- SeparatePosition(join_teosinte)
teosinte_p <- as.data.frame(results[1])
teosinte_up <- as.data.frame(results[2])
teosinte_mp <- as.data.frame(results[3])

colnames(teosinte_up) <- header2
colnames(teosinte_mp) <- header2

write_delim(teosinte_up, file = paste0("Output_files/teosinte_up.csv"), delim = "\t")
write_delim(teosinte_mp, file = paste0("Output_files/teosinte_mp.csv"), delim = "\t")

species <- "teosinte"
OutputFile(teosinte_p)

```

## Visualization

Distribution of SNPs on and across chromosomes. Note that this is the total number of SNP positions that were investigated and not the actual number of SNPs.
```{r}
NofSNP <- join_maize %>% filter(!.[[2]] %in% c("Chromosome")) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=.[[2]])) + geom_bar(fill="darkgreen") + labs(title="Number of SNPs per Chromosome",x="Chromosome number",y="Counts") + geom_text(aes(label = after_stat(count)), stat = "count", vjust = -0.5)

plot(NofSNP)
ggsave("Graphs/NofSNP.png",NofSNP)
```

Compare SNPs in maize and teosinte. _It seems that all SNP positions are truly polymorphic for both maize and teosinte that the graphs are the same. I will keep working on this_
```{r}
# create a new data frame by copying the original and removing header, so all changes made in visualization is not changing the original data
join_maize_visual <- join_maize %>% filter(!.[[2]] %in% c("Chromosome"))
join_maize_visual[join_maize_visual == "?/?"] <- NA 

df <- join_maize_visual %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(is_SNP = n_distinct(c_across(4:ncol(join_maize_visual)), na.rm = TRUE) == 1) %>%
  ungroup()

maize_true_snp <- df %>% filter(is_SNP %in% c("FALSE")) %>%
  count(.[[2]]) %>% ggplot(aes(x=factor(.[[1]]),y=n)) + geom_col(fill="lightblue") + labs(title="SNP sites per Chromosome in maize",x="Chromosome number",y="Counts")

plot(maize_true_snp)
ggsave("Graphs/maize_true_snp.png",maize_true_snp)

# repeat for teosinte
join_teosinte_visual <- join_teosinte %>% filter(!.[[2]] %in% c("Chromosome"))
join_teosinte_visual[join_teosinte_visual == "?/?"] <- NA 

df <- join_teosinte_visual %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(is_SNP = n_distinct(c_across(4:ncol(join_teosinte_visual)), na.rm = TRUE) == 1) %>%
  ungroup()

teosinte_true_snp <- df %>% filter(is_SNP %in% c("FALSE")) %>%
  count(.[[2]]) %>% ggplot(aes(x=factor(.[[1]]),y=n)) + geom_col(fill="lightyellow") + labs(title="SNP sites per Chromosome in teosinte",x="Chromosome number",y="Counts")

plot(teosinte_true_snp)
ggsave("Graphs/teosinte_true_snp.png",teosinte_true_snp)

```

Missing data and amount of heterozygosity
```{r}
# convert to long format
long_maize <- join_maize_visual %>% pivot_longer(cols = 4:ncol(join_maize_visual),
               names_to = "sample_ID",
               values_to = "genotype")

# classify genotype type to missing, homozygous, and heterozygous 
long_maize <- long_maize %>%
  mutate(genotype_type = case_when(is.na(genotype) ~ "missing",genotype %in%
                                     c("A/A","T/T","C/C","G/G") ~ "homozygous", 
                                   TRUE ~ "heterozygous"))

# calculate proportion of genotype types in each sample
genotype_prop <- long_maize %>%
  group_by(sample_ID, genotype_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(sample_ID) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n))

# plot genotype type proportion per sample
type_sample_maize <- ggplot(genotype_prop, aes(x = sample_ID, y = prop, fill = genotype_type)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Maize: genotype type proportion per sample", x = "Sample_ID",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "Genotype")
plot(type_sample_maize)
ggsave("Graphs/type_sample_maize.png",type_sample_maize)

# plot genotype type proportion per chromosome across samples
colnames(long_maize)[2] <- "chromosome"
genotype_prop2 <- long_maize %>%
  group_by(chromosome, genotype_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(chromosome) %>%
  mutate(prop2 = n / sum(n))

type_chr_maize <- ggplot(genotype_prop2, aes(x = chromosome, y = prop2, fill = genotype_type)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Maize: genotype type proportion per chromosome",x = "Chromosome",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "Genotype")
plot(type_chr_maize)
ggsave("Graphs/type_chr_maize.png",type_chr_maize)

```

Repeat for teosinte
```{r}
# convert to long format
long_teosinte <- join_teosinte_visual %>% pivot_longer(cols = 4:ncol(join_teosinte_visual),
               names_to = "sample_ID",
               values_to = "genotype")

# classify genotype type to missing, homozygous, and heterozygous 
long_teosinte <- long_teosinte %>%
  mutate(genotype_type = case_when(is.na(genotype) ~ "missing",genotype %in%
                                     c("A/A","T/T","C/C","G/G") ~ "homozygous", 
                                   TRUE ~ "heterozygous"))

# calculate proportion of genotype types in each sample
genotype_prop <- long_teosinte %>%
  group_by(sample_ID, genotype_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(sample_ID) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n))

# plot genotype type proportion per sample
type_sample_teosinte <- ggplot(genotype_prop, aes(x = sample_ID, y = prop, 
                                                  fill = genotype_type)) + geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Teosinte: genotype type proportion per sample", x = "Sample_ID",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "Genotype")
plot(type_sample_teosinte)
ggsave("Graphs/type_sample_teosinte.png",type_sample_teosinte)

# plot genotype type proportion per chromosome across samples
colnames(long_teosinte)[2] <- "chromosome"
genotype_prop2 <- long_teosinte %>%
  group_by(chromosome, genotype_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(chromosome) %>%
  mutate(prop2 = n / sum(n))

type_chr_teosinte <- ggplot(genotype_prop2, aes(x = chromosome, y = prop2, 
                                                fill = genotype_type)) + geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Teosinte: genotype type proportion per chromosome",x = "Chromosome",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "Genotype")
plot(type_chr_teosinte)
ggsave("Graphs/type_chr_teosinte.png",type_chr_teosinte)

```

```{R Sam Kiuna diagnosis}
# inspecting your output files
maize_chr2_in <- read_csv("Output_files/maize_chr2_in.csv")
teosinte_chr10_de <- read_csv("Output_files/teosinte_chr10_de.csv")

View(maize_chr2_in)
View(teosinte_chr10_de)

ncol(maize_chr2_in)
ncol(teosinte_chr10_de)


```
